entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The main subsistence crop for smallholder farmers studied for productivity and water use efficiency.

entities.name: SOWING DENSITY
entities.description: The number of plants per unit area, tested at low and high levels to observe effects on yield and water use.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The yield output of the crop, which increased with higher sowing density under specific conditions.

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ratio of biomass produced to the amount of water used by the crop.

entities.name: HIGH EVAPORATIVE DEMAND
entities.description: Environmental conditions characterized by high water loss, influencing the response of crops to sowing density.

entities.name: GENOTYPIC VARIATION
entities.description: The difference in response to sowing density among different genotypes of pearl millet.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Trials conducted in open fields in India and Senegal to test yield response to density.

entities.name: LYSIMETRIC EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Experiments using lysimeters to precisely measure water use and water use efficiency.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: One of the geographical sites where the field and lysimetric trials were conducted.

entities.name: SENEGAL
entities.description: One of the geographical sites where the field and lysimetric trials were conducted.

entities.name: VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: A measure of the difference between the amount of moisture the air can hold and how much it currently holds, decreased by high sowing density.

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total biological mass produced by the crop, which increased proportionally more than water use under high density.

entities.name: WATER USE
entities.description: The total amount of water consumed by the crop during growth.

relationships.description: Management practice applied to the crop to increase productivity
Higher sowing density significantly increased yield in all genotypes
Higher sowing density increased the water use efficiency of the crops
High-density treatment decreased the vapor pressure deficit in the canopies
Higher density treatment increased the total water use
Higher density treatment increased the resulting biomass
Higher sowing density increased yield specifically when trials were carried out in high evaporative demand conditions
The link between yield increase and WUE increase was tighter in high evaporative demand seasons
Trials were carried out in India
Trials were carried out in Senegal
Used to measure the water use efficiency of the crop
Used to measure the water use of the crop
Water use is a primary component used to calculate water use efficiency
Biomass production is a primary component used to calculate water use efficiency
The study assessed possible genotypic variation in response to increased sowing density
